content: {"question": "What evidence thresholds would FDA, EFSA, and Health Canada require to approve an authorized health claim for intermittent fasting (e.g., 'may aid weight management' or 'may improve glycemic control'), and which specific adverse-event signals (appendicular lean mass loss, hypoglycemia in insulin-users, eating-disorder precipitation) would trigger a consumer advisory under current regulatory frameworks?", "search_queries": ["FDA qualified health claim intermittent fasting evidence standard", "EFSA Panel Dietetic Products reduction disease risk claim criteria", "Health Canada food directorate dietary guidance intermittent fasting", "intermittent fasting adverse events hypoglycemia insulin user regulatory", "eating disorder risk time-restricted eating regulatory advisory"], "perspective": "Regulatory"}
